raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening or reframing the contribution claim and thesis above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft makes a causal and evidential argument that would be expected to hold together logically under an improvement request.","R1.3":"Several claims about calibration, distribution shift, and deployment depend on whether the reported results actually support them.","R1.5":"The draft's narrative arc from method to evidence to conclusion is part of what a general draft improvement request reasonably covers.","R1.6":"A broad request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, concision, flow, and register load-bearing responsibilities.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what the artifact does and does not establish.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong claims, so avoiding inflated conclusions is an always-active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or rewriting the draft.","RX.4":"Because the draft gestures toward deployment, downstream harm from overstated reliability must remain in scope.","RX.5":"Responsibility assignments should remain traceable to the delegation wording and artifact content."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening or reframing the contribution claim and thesis above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes a causal and evidential argument that would be expected to hold together logically under an improvement request.
rationale.R1.3: Several claims about calibration, distribution shift, and deployment depend on whether the reported results actually support them.
rationale.R1.5: The draft's narrative arc from method to evidence to conclusion is part of what a general draft improvement request reasonably covers.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, concision, flow, and register load-bearing responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what the artifact does and does not establish.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong claims, so avoiding inflated conclusions is an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or rewriting the draft.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft gestures toward deployment, downstream harm from overstated reliability must remain in scope.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility assignments should remain traceable to the delegation wording and artifact content.
